Okay, so here's what we covered in Module 2. We covered the difference between the front end and the back end, the purpose of both the front end and the back end, how the A players in marketing view each, how they view the front end and the back end. We talked about the three objectives of your front end offer. We talked about front end product objectives, the actual objectives of the product itself, not the offer. Then we talked about how to set up the sale of your first back end offer and we finished by talking about how to actually build your product with the hook in mind. Here's what to do now. First, begin brainstorming ideas for your front end product, taking into account market place sophistication, what the market has seen, what they're being offered right now, and begin brainstorming ideas for your front end product based on your hook and promise and the elements that we talked about. Begin jotting down ideas for how and when you'll use the pre-selling techniques in your front end product to set up the sale and create the desire for your first back end product, the first offer that you're going to make to new customers after their front end purchase. Then finally, of course, begin thinking about what that first back end offer and product is going to be that you will make to customers who, again, purchase your front end product.
